title: Dysphania dunosa
taxonomy: Dysphania dunosa is classified in the family Amaranthaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the pigweed family. This family is part of the order Caryophyllales, which includes other flowering plants like the carnations and the pinks. The genus Dysphania is a group of plants within the Amaranthaceae family, and it is often used to describe plants that were previously classified as Chenopodium. Dysphania dunosa is a species within this genus and is native to Australia and other parts of the world. The classification of Dysphania dunosa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Amaranthaceae family.

intro: Dysphania ambrosioides (also known as Dysphania dunosa) is a type of annual or biennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Chenopodiaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dysphania ambrosioides include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Dysphania ambrosioides is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
